text: Shodan - Comprehensive Testing Guide for Red Teaming, VAPT, and Reconnaissance

Shodan is a powerful search engine that lets you find specific devices connected to the internet, discover which of your devices are publicly accessible, and identify vulnerabilities in your infrastructure. Unlike traditional search engines that crawl websites, Shodan scans internet-connected devices and services.
# Find specific IoT devices (e.g., Raspberry Pi)
# Target specific geographic coordinates within range (in km)
# Target specific IPs (specific IP or CIDR notation)
# Find services with specific vulnerability tags
# Find routers with common web interfaces

Section 7: Practical Shodan Web UI Workflows
Start with basic organizational information (name, domains, IP ranges)
Search for the organization name: org:"Target Organization"
Note down discovered IP ranges, domains, and technologies
Use "Details" view to examine banner information for version data
Search for known CVEs affecting discovered services
Use the vuln: filter with specific CVE identifiers
Look for common misconfigurations in the discovered services
Use banner information to identify outdated software versions
Log in to your Shodan account
Click on "Collections" in the top navigation
Create a new collection for your target organization
Add relevant search queries to your collection
Review and export collection data as needed
Create alerts for your target organization's assets
Configure alert triggers (new vulnerabilities, new services)
Add geographic filters to your search
Click on the "Maps" view to visualize results geographically
Identify geographic clusters of target infrastructure
Use the map to refine location-based searches
Click on the "Images" view to see visual captures
Look for login pages, admin panels, and monitoring interfaces
Perform broad organization search: org:"Target Organization"
Use the "Download" button to export results (requires premium account)
Group assets by service type (web, mail, database, etc.)
Create tables of exposed services and versions
Prioritize findings based on exposure and criticality
Note potential false positives for verification
Create specific queries for each asset category
Include version information in queries where possible
Set up alerts for new assets appearing in searches
Set up alerts for new vulnerabilities in existing assets
Test alert system by creating a controlled change
Schedule weekly/monthly reviews of Shodan data
Update queries as the target environment evolves
Only scan targets within authorized scope
Respect terms of service for Shodan and target systems
Do not attempt to exploit vulnerabilities without permission
Report critical findings to affected organizations
Use Shodan's passive scanning capabilities instead of active scanning
Distribute queries over time to avoid rate-limiting
Use premium API access for more discreet data collection
Avoid downloading large datasets in short timeframes
Start broad, then narrow with specific filters
Use negative filters to exclude false positives
Use quotes for exact matching on critical terms
Use parentheses for complex logical operations
Limit results with country and organization filters first
Use regex where supported for pattern matching
Save effective search patterns as named queries
Create a free account at https://shodan.io
Consider upgrading to a premium account for:
Configure your profile settings and API key
Use the filters dropdown menu to add common filters
Use the "Explore" section to discover trending searches
Use browser bookmarks to save effective search queries
Enable HTTPS for all Shodan interactions
Domain Tools: Use with Shodan to expand target scope
Vulnerability Databases: Reference for discovered versions
Identify organization name, domains, IP ranges
Create initial search queries in Shodan
Document found assets in tracking system
Look for unusual or unique services
Regular Updates: Infrastructure changes frequently; regularly refresh searches
Context Matters: Understand what you're looking at before drawing conclusions
Verification: Always verify findings with additional tools/methods
Documentation: Keep detailed notes of search queries and results
Continuous Learning: Stay updated on new Shodan features and search techniques
